addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

addedBlocks:
## claude.ai Figma
The official Figma MCP server. Use this server whenever the user wants to create, generate, edit, implement, or sync any design, UI, screen, component, mockup, or visual — in Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or Figma Slides — and whenever the user mentions Figma or provides a figma.com URL.

This server bridges code and design in both directions, and supports designing from scratch using existing design systems and codebases.

CAPABILITIES:
- Read designs FROM Figma into code (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Write designs INTO Figma from code, intent, or existing components (use_figma, generate_figma_design, create_new_file, upload_assets)
- Bridge code and design via Code Connect (get_code_connect_map, add_code_connect_map)
- Create diagrams and FigJam content (generate_diagram, get_figjam)

WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user wants to create, mock up, or generate any UI, screen, component, or design — even if Figma isn't named
- The user wants to implement a design as code (design-to-code)
- The user wants to push a page, view, or component into Figma (code-to-design)
- The user wants to update, sync, or edit an existing Figma file
- The user shares a figma.com URL
- The user wants to build or extend a design system, design tokens, or component library
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

SKILLS (Prefer the skills shipped with the Figma plugin on the agent being used. If no Figma plugin is installed, use the skills served by the Figma MCP resource):
- /figma-use — MANDATORY before calling use_figma; fallback: skill://figma/figma-use/SKILL.md
- /figma-generate-design — for translating an app page or layout into Figma; fallback: skill://figma/figma-generate-design/SKILL.md
- /figma-generate-library — for building a design system in Figma from code; fallback: skill://figma/figma-generate-library/SKILL.md
- /figma-code-connect — for mapping Figma components to codebase components; fallback: skill://figma/figma-code-connect/SKILL.md
- /figma-use-figjam — for FigJam-specific use_f… [truncated]

My lexical relevance filter flagged 56% of the corpus but had false positives (it flagged a T1 Acemoglu-class paper). Built the AUTHORIZED part instead: §-1.3.1 class (a) chrome non-sources.
8	- 2026-07-12 | Rank1 (539d1a5) | ROOT CAUSE, one line: `detect_content_integrity_junk`'s ZYTE-RECOVERY GUARD (`if len(body)>=200: return (False,"")`) sits ABOVE the bot-challenge TITLE screen, making it unreachable for any row with a real-sized body. But the deeper miss is the BODY detector: `classify_block_page` caught **0 of 52** ResearchGate challenge cards ("## Security check required / Ray ID / Client IP") because RG's card carries none of the classic Cloudflare wording. FIX: 2 AND-pair visible rules. Validated on the real corpus BEFORE landing: 52/52 recall, **0 false positives across all 1023 rows**, 0 T1/T2/T3 touched. +2 tests.
9	- 2026-07-12 | Rank2 (887e5f8) | DEEPER ROOT CAUSE: `junk_deletion_gate.partition_rows` — the §-1.3.1 run-level chrome deletion, DEFAULT-ON, 18 passing tests — was **called by NOTHING in src/**. Dead code since it landed. And `is_row_content_junk` only READ a `content_integrity_junk` stamp written solely by the fetch-path fold-in, so a PRE-BUILT corpus (how the baseline was produced) carries no stamp and the gate no-ops on every row. FIX: wired partition_rows into the evidence_pool seam next to the retraction gate + stamp-first/detector-fallback predicate. **Real corpus: 53 deleted (52 T7 cards + 1 unrecoverable), 944 kept, ZERO T1/T2/T3.** Kill-switch byte-identical, idempotent. +3 tests.
10	- 2026-07-12 | FABLE GATE | Verdict PASS-WITH-FIX. Confirmed no dangling-ev_id bug (outline plans predate the seam but every consumer is membership-guarded; bibliography only draws from actually-cited rows). Found a latent hard-drop I missed → Rank2b.
11	- 2026-07-12 | Rank2b (beb05a4) | Applied all Fable findings. **The dangerous one:** partition_rows also has an OFF-TOPIC arm (default-ON), and I passed `fresh_off_subject_ids=None`, which leaves the stale-stamp fence un-enforced — so reusing a pre-built corpus_snapshot under a DIFFERENT question would hard-delete credible on-topic sources on a foreign verdict (the exact false hard-drop §-1.3.1(b) forbids). Reproduced it, fixed with `fresh_off_subject_ids=()`. Also: key-preserving pool rebuild; junk_disclosed now actually reaches Methods + manifest (§-1.3.1 requires the count+reason, it was dying on the result object); corrected a false single-source-of-truth docstring claim. +1 test.
12	- 2026-07-12 | BACKUP (retry) | GitHub push STILL BLOCKED: no `gh` CLI, no ~/.git-credentials, no credential.helper, no PAT anywhere on box (scanned). Re-made local bundle: /home/polaris/polaris_project/backups_flywheel-v1.bundle (175MB, all refs). **OPERATOR ACTION NEEDED: supply a GitHub PAT/SSH key — offsite backup is impossible without one.**
13	- 2026-07-12 | VERIFY-RUN | Re-ran outline-only (PG_STOP_AFTER_ROUTED_OUTLINE=1) on the same corpus/task-72 with the Rank1/2/2b junk gate live, to confirm the 52 chrome rows are gone end-to-end.
14	- 2026-07-12 | Rank3 DIAGNOSIS | Grounding read of the baseline outline found the next structural pathology: **the residual catch-all "Additional Corroborated Findings" absorbs 545 of 1032 rows (53% of the corpus)** vs 14-108 for each real thematic section. Traced to `verified_compose.route_orphan_baskets_to_section_plans` (log proves it: "routed 963 orphan basket(s) + 0 unassigned singleton(s)", so ALL of it came through the BASKET leg, not the weighted_enrichment producer). Routing = argmax of RAW content-word intersection between the basket's key (`_basket_topic_words` = claim_text+subject+predicate ONLY) and a plan's key (title+focus ONLY, a ~15-22 word vocabulary); zero overlap => dumped. Measured: **329 of the 545 dumped rows DO overlap a real section when scored on their OWN title+quote** — they are not topically homeless, the routing KEY is just too thin (exact lexical intersection over a tiny vocab: "jobs" misses "labor", "employment" misses "work"). Simulated richer key: residual 545 -> 216, every thematic section ~doubles its evidence. This is a pure-PLACEMENT defect (keep-all, nothing deleted, faithfulness engine untouched) and it plausibly drives the known depth gap (our ~4k words vs competitors' 10-22k). Fable gate running before any change lands.
15	- 2026-07-12 | FABLE GATE (Rank3) | Verdict **PASS-WITH-FIX**, and it CORRECTED MY DIAGNOSIS — my "329 of 545 would route" was inflated. Fable measured that **174 of those 329 are argmax TIES on a single generic word** ("research"/"effects"/"studies"): e.g. "[PDF] An Annotated Bibliography of Privacy-Violating Attacks" matches Introduction on exactly `{'research'}`. It audited all 545: only **195 carry any labor/AI/employment topical anchor**, 56 are block pages, **294 have NO topical anchor at all** (climate-risk IMF WPs, privacy bibliographies, school infrastructure, Quora). **Most of the residual is off-topic junk being CORRECTLY quarantined, not stranded evidence** — the F1 docstring says so by design (verified_compose.py:3812-3815). My "545->216, sections double" claim was WRONG. A hotter lexical router would smear captcha + climate-policy rows into thematic sections, and **strict_verify would NOT stop them** (it blocks unfaithful sentences, not off-topic FAITHFUL ones) — it would make the report worse.
16	  - Deeper causes I missed: (i) the magnet is the **tie-break**, not title length — `overlap > best_overlap` (vc:3864-3866) hands every tie to plan[0] = Introduction; (ii) **plan-side enrichment (my alternative) is decisively WORSE** — simulated, it routes 285/545 into Introduction, zero-overlap drops to 0, quarantine destroyed, and it routes literal "Just a moment..." pages into thematic sections; (iii) normalization (Jaccard) does NOT fix the magnet, it just moves it (GenAI/Conclusions become the new attractors, still on 1-word junk matches); (iv) `BasketMember` has **no title field** (credibility_pass.py:1504-1526), so the richer key REQUIRES threading `evidence_pool` in; member `direct_quote` is deliberately empty fail-closed and only folded for SUPPORTS members, so **row title+statement from the pool is the high-value addition**, not quotes.
17	  - **THE REAL LEVER for the residual's size is NOT a hotter router:** the §-1.3.1 semantic topic judge fired **ZERO times this run** (log: "DELETED 0") because nothing wrote an affirmative OFF_SUBJECT stamp — the retrieval-time topic gate only DEMOTE-keeps. Arming that judge is the operator-sanctioned lever (§-1.3.1(b), judge-only, fail-open). Queued as Rank4.
18	  - GATED SPEC for Rank3 (what will actually land): basket key += member row **title+statement** resolved from `evidence_pool`; scorer = raw overlap with **min_overlap >= 2** (mirrors the module's own `_DEFAULT_REPAIR_MIN_OVERLAP = 2`, vc:3384), tie-break overlap/|pw| then section order; **Intro / Cross-Study / Conclusions excluded as orphan TARGETS** (connective sections must not absorb primary evidence — note `_is_scaffolding_section_title` does NOT cover them, it matches render scaffolding only, so a new predicate is needed); residual keep-all + judge-only delete leg byte-unchanged; new kill-switch default-OFF. Honest expectation: **~100-150 rows correctly rehomed, ~400 stay in residual** — that remainder is the true state of this corpus.
19	
20	## Net so far
21	**52 failed fetches (Cloudflare/ResearchGate challenge cards) were grounding report prose as citable T7 "sources"; they are now deleted and disclosed.** The faithfulness engine could never have caught this — strict_verify checks sentence↔span fidelity, and a sentence *can* be entailed by the text of a challenge card, so chrome passed the only hard gate and still grounded prose on nothing.
22	
23	Regression: 75 failed / 2269 passed vs **77 / 2266 at pre-flywheel baseline (0e0eca9)** — zero new failures; the 75 are pre-existing and unrelated (audit_ir registry + import-path collection errors).
24	
25	## Next
26	- Re-run outline-only (PG_STOP_AFTER_ROUTED_OUTLINE=1) to confirm the 52 rows are gone end-to-end, then Fable-gate the new outline for richness before any compose.
27	- Open quality gap (still unaddressed, from the laptop handoff): verified MATH/STAT synthesis (the outliner collects numbers but does not compute them), and report length ~4k words vs competitors' 10-22k.
28	- The 4 sciencedirect rows share the stale "Just a moment..." TITLE but have genuinely recovered bodies — 3 are correctly KEPT, but their **title is still chrome** and will render into the bibliography as "Just a moment...". Title repair (NOT deletion) is a separate small step.
29

diff --git a/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py b/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py
index 35df491..1e62176 100644
--- a/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py
+++ b/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py
@@ -9625,6 +9625,14 @@ async def generate_multi_section_report(
     # requirements block, lets required titles govern validation, and conforms the plan set.
     deliverable_spec: Any = None,
     scope_spec: Any = None,
+    # FLYWHEEL Rank4 (§-1.3.1(b)): the evidence_ids a SEMANTIC topic judge affirmatively verdicted
+    # OFF_SUBJECT **on THIS run, against THIS research_question**. This is the fresh-verdict fence the
+    # pool seam's off-topic arm requires: None (the default) => the seam passes ``()`` exactly as it
+    # does today => the off-topic arm stays inert by construction and ONLY the chrome arm can delete
+    # => byte-identical. A caller may arm the arm ONLY by judging the corpus against the SAME question
+    # the report is composed for (a verdict from another question is a foreign verdict — the false
+    # hard-drop §-1.3.1(b) forbids). NEVER populate this from tier, lexeme, or a breadth number.
+    fresh_off_subject_ids: set[str] | None = None,
     model: Optional[str] = None,
     outline_temperature: float = 0.2,
     section_temperature: float = 0.3,
@@ -10090,9 +10098,25 @@ async def generate_multi_section_report(
     # seam has no fresh verdict in scope, so its off-topic arm stays inert by construction and
     # ONLY the chrome arm can delete here. (Operators who want stamp-deletion can still set
     # PG_DELETE_OFFTOPIC_FRESH_VERDICT_ONLY=0 — an explicit choice, not a silent default.)
+    #
+    # FLYWHEEL Rank4: ``fresh_off_subject_ids`` is now THREADED (it was hardcoded ``()``). The fence
+    # above is UNCHANGED in spirit: None (no judge ran) still collapses to the empty concrete set, so
+    # the off-topic arm stays inert and only chrome can delete. It arms ONLY when a caller supplies a
+    # FRESH judge verdict set produced against THIS run's research_question. Empty set (judge ran and
+    # confirmed nothing) is also inert — deleting requires an affirmative OFF_SUBJECT id, never an
+    # absence of evidence.
+    _fresh_off_subject: set[str] = {
+        str(_e) for _e in (fresh_off_subject_ids or ()) if str(_e or "")
+    }
+    # The router leg (below) historically called the predicate with fresh_off_subject_ids=None
+    # (freshness UN-enforced). Keep that EXACT behaviour when no judge ran (byte-identical), and
+    # enforce the same fence as the pool seam only once a caller actually arms one.
+    _router_off_subject_fence: set[str] | None = (
+        _fresh_off_subject if fresh_off_subject_ids is not None else None
+    )
     _pool_rows_pre_junk = list(evidence_pool.values())
     _kept_rows, _junk_rows = junk_deletion_gate.partition_rows(
-        _pool_rows_pre_junk, fresh_off_subject_ids=(),
+        _pool_rows_pre_junk, fresh_off_subject_ids=_fresh_off_subject,
     )
     junk_disclosed: list[dict[str, Any]] = list(_junk_rows)
     if _junk_rows:
@@ -10667,7 +10691,13 @@ async def generate_multi_section_report(
                     continue
                 # (a) fail-open confirmed-off-topic set (affirmative OFF_SUBJECT only; positive
                 #     relevance vetoes; any uncertainty/error => row NOT added => KEEP).
-                if is_row_deletable_offtopic(_row):
+                # FLYWHEEL Rank4: pass the SAME fresh-verdict fence the pool seam uses. This leg used
+                # to call the predicate with NO fence, so a STALE ``topic_off_subject`` stamp carried
+                # in on a reloaded corpus_snapshot could delete a basket here even though the pool
+                # seam above (fenced) had correctly KEPT that very row — the two seams disagreed. With
+                # a fresh set threaded, both seams enforce the same §-1.3.1(b) fresh-verdict-only rule.
+                # UNARMED (caller passed None) => pass None => legacy behaviour, byte-identical.
+                if is_row_deletable_offtopic(_row, fresh_off_subject_ids=_router_off_subject_fence):
                     _off_topic_ev_ids.add(_eid)
                     continue
                 # (b) unassigned high-tier singleton candidate (not reachable by any section yet).
Shell cwd was reset to /home/polaris/polaris_project

+                # seam above (fenced) had correctly KEPT that very row — the two seams disagreed. With
+                # a fresh set threaded, both seams enforce the same §-1.3.1(b) fresh-verdict-only rule.

diff --git a/scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py b/scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py
index ac1d615..cee8296 100644
--- a/scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py
+++ b/scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py
@@ -121,6 +121,201 @@ def _audit_citations(report_text: str, biblio: list[dict]) -> dict:
     }
 
 
+# ── FLYWHEEL Rank4 — ARM THE SEMANTIC TOPIC JUDGE ON A PRE-BUILT CORPUS ─────────────────────────
+#
+# THE GAP (measured, not theorised): the §-1.3.1(b) off-topic DELETE leg has never fired on this
+# path. `is_row_deletable_offtopic` keys on an AFFIRMATIVE ``topic_off_subject`` stamp, and the ONLY
+# writer of that stamp is ``classify_topic_relevance`` — which runs at RETRIEVAL time (the live
+# sweep) and is called by NOTHING on the pre-built-corpus path. The corpus carries 633
+# ``topic_offtopic_demoted`` labels but ZERO ``topic_off_subject`` stamps, so the run logged
+# "DELETED 0 judge-confirmed off-topic item(s)" and ~294 alien sources (climate-risk IMF working
+# papers, privacy-attack bibliographies, school-infrastructure pages, content-marketing stats) sat in
+# the grounding pool, eligible to be CITED in an AI-labour literature review. The faithfulness engine
+# cannot catch them: strict_verify checks span fidelity, not topicality — a sentence grounded on a
+# climate working paper is perfectly FAITHFUL and perfectly off-topic.
+#
+# THE FIX: ARM the judge that already exists. We do NOT build a new one, and we do NOT lexically
+# guess. §-1.3.1(b) admits exactly one delete trigger — an affirmative SEMANTIC judge verdict,
+# FAIL-OPEN. Everything below is fail-open scaffolding around that one call:
+#   * The judge is run against ``rq`` — the SAME (DRB-override) question the report is composed and
+#     scored against. Judging the corpus's own RQ would produce a FOREIGN verdict (the false
+#     hard-drop §-1.3.1(b) forbids, and the exact landmine Rank2b defused).
+#   * TOKEN-STARVATION TRAP (the silent no-op): ``PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_MAX_TOKENS`` defaults to 1200, but
+#     the judge model is reasoning-first — it burns the budget on hidden reasoning, truncates before
+#     emitting its verdict lines, returns empty content, and EVERY batch fails open. The judge would
+#     "run", bill real money, and delete nothing. We raise the floor to 4000 and FAIL LOUD if every
+#     batch failed open (that is an outage, not a verdict of "all clean").
+#   * BLAST-RADIUS CEILING: if the judge confirms OFF_SUBJECT on more than PG_TOPIC_DELETE_MAX_FRACTION
+#     (default 0.40) of judged rows, we delete NOTHING and log LOUD. A well-formed but WRONG batch
+#     response (index rotation, prompt injection from a snippet) is the one failure the parser cannot
+#     catch; this is the containment. It can only ever REFUSE deletion — it can never force a number
+#     up, so it is not a banned breadth knob.
+#   * T1/T2 DOUBLE-CONFIRMATION (not exemption): a seminal paper wrongly verdicted OFF_SUBJECT is the
+#     costliest false delete. A blanket high-tier exemption would use tier as a RELEVANCE proxy and
+#     would shield precisely the credible-tier junk this audit found (the IMF climate WPs are T1/T2).
+#     So tier is NOT a keep-veto; it is an ESCALATION criterion: a T1/T2 row the batch judge marks
+#     OFF_SUBJECT is RE-judged alone on its full quote, and deletes only on a SECOND affirmative
+#     verdict. Nobody is kept by tier and nobody is deleted by tier — the bar is just higher where a
+#     mistake costs most.
+# Default-OFF (``PG_PREBUILT_TOPIC_JUDGE``): unset => returns None => the generator passes ``()`` at
+# the pool seam exactly as today => byte-identical.
+_TOPIC_JUDGE_ENV = "PG_PREBUILT_TOPIC_JUDGE"
+_TOPIC_DELETE_MAX_FRACTION_ENV = "PG_TOPIC_DELETE_MAX_FRACTION"
+
+
+def _topic_judge_llm(model: str, max_tokens: int):
+    """Synchronous ``str -> str`` LLM bridge for the judge (mirrors the live sweep's `_topic_llm`).
+
+    The judge is a pure sync function by design (unit-testable with a stub), so the async
+    OpenRouter client is driven on a worker thread with its own event loop."""
+    import asyncio as _asyncio  # noqa: PLC0415
+    import concurrent.futures as _futures  # noqa: PLC0415
+
+    def _call(prompt: str) -> str:
+        from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient  # noqa: PLC0415
+
+        async def _run() -> str:
+            client = OpenRouterClient(model=model)
+            try:
+                resp = await client.generate(
+                    prompt=prompt, max_tokens=max_tokens, temperature=0.0,
+                )
+                return (resp.content or "").strip()
+            finally:
+                if hasattr(client, "close"):
+                    try:
+                        await client.close()
+                    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
+                        pass
+
+        with _futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1) as pool:
+            return pool.submit(lambda: _asyncio.run(_run())).result()
+
+    return _call
+
+
+def _arm_topic_judge(evidence: list, rq: str, run_dir: Path, log) -> "set[str] | None":
+    """Run the semantic topic judge over a PRE-BUILT corpus; return the FRESH OFF_SUBJECT id set.
+
+    Returns None when the flag is OFF (=> the generator's off-topic arm stays inert, byte-identical).
+    Returns a (possibly empty) concrete set when the judge ran. FAIL-OPEN everywhere: any exception,
+    any starved/unparseable batch, or a tripped blast-radius ceiling yields an EMPTY set — which
+    deletes NOTHING. Deletion requires an affirmative verdict; never an absence of one."""
+    if os.getenv(_TOPIC_JUDGE_ENV, "0").strip().lower() in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no"):
+        return None
+
+    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import PG_GENERATOR_MODEL  # noqa: PLC0415
+    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.topic_relevance_gate import (  # noqa: PLC0415
+        classify_topic_relevance, mark_topic_judge_ran,
+    )
+
+    model = os.getenv("PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_MODEL", "").strip() or PG_GENERATOR_MODEL
+    # The starvation floor. A reasoning-first judge at 1200 truncates before its verdict lines.
+    try:
+        max_tokens = int(os.getenv("PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_MAX_TOKENS", "").strip() or "4000")
+    except ValueError:
+        max_tokens = 4000
+    max_tokens = max(max_tokens, 4000)
+    llm = _topic_judge_llm(model, max_tokens)
+
+    t0 = time.time()
+    log.info("[topic-judge] ARMED (§-1.3.1(b)): judging %d rows against the COMPOSED rq "
+             "(model=%s max_tokens=%d). Fail-open: only an affirmative OFF_SUBJECT deletes.",
+             len(evidence), model, max_tokens)
+    try:
+        result = classify_topic_relevance(evidence, rq, llm)
+    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a judge crash must NEVER delete anything
+        log.error("[topic-judge] judge FAILED (%s) — deleting NOTHING (fail-open).", exc)
+        return set()
+    mark_topic_judge_ran()
+
+    fresh = {
+        str(r.get("evidence_id", "") or "")
+        for r in (result.demoted_rows or [])
+        if isinstance(r, dict) and r.get("topic_off_subject") is True
+    }
+    fresh.discard("")
+
+    # FAIL-LOUD: the judge ran but confirmed literally nothing across ~1000 rows of a corpus we KNOW
+    # carries alien sources. That is far more likely an outage (starved/failed batches) than a clean
+    # corpus. Say so; do not silently report success.
+    if not fresh:
+        log.error("[topic-judge] judge returned ZERO OFF_SUBJECT verdicts over %d rows. Treat as a "
+                  "SUSPECTED OUTAGE (starved reasoning budget / failed batches), NOT as 'corpus is "
+                  "clean'. Deleting nothing.", result.n_in)
+        _write_topic_disclosure(run_dir, result, fresh, "zero_verdicts_suspected_outage", model, t0)
+        return set()
+
+    # T1/T2 DOUBLE-CONFIRMATION — escalate, never exempt.
+    escalated_kept: list[str] = []
+    by_id = {str(r.get("evidence_id", "") or ""): r for r in evidence if isinstance(r, dict)}
+    for eid in sorted(fresh):
+        row = by_id.get(eid) or {}
+        if str(row.get("tier", "") or "").strip().upper() not in ("T1", "T2"):
+            continue
+        try:
+            confirm = classify_topic_relevance([dict(row)], rq, llm)
+        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — uncertainty => KEEP
+            log.warning("[topic-judge] T1/T2 re-judge errored for %s (%s) — KEEPING (fail-open).",
+                        eid, str(exc)[:80])
+            escalated_kept.append(eid)
+            continue
+        still_off = any(
+            isinstance(r, dict) and r.get("topic_off_subject") is True
+            for r in (confirm.demoted_rows or [])
+        )
+        if not still_off:
+            escalated_kept.append(eid)
+    if escalated_kept:
+        log.warning("[topic-judge] T1/T2 escalation RESCUED %d high-tier row(s) the batch judge had "
+                    "marked OFF_SUBJECT (second verdict did not confirm): %s",
+                    len(escalated_kept), ", ".join(escalated_kept[:12]))
+        fresh -= set(escalated_kept)
+
+    # BLAST-RADIUS CEILING — containment for a well-formed-but-wrong judge response.
+    try:
+        max_frac = float(os.getenv(_TOPIC_DELETE_MAX_FRACTION_ENV, "").strip() or "0.40")
+    except ValueError:
+        max_frac = 0.40
+    judged = max(1, int(result.n_in or len(evidence)))
+    frac = len(fresh) / judged
+    if frac > max_frac:
+        log.error("[topic-judge] BLAST-RADIUS CEILING TRIPPED: judge confirmed OFF_SUBJECT on %d/%d "
+                  "rows (%.1f%% > %.1f%% ceiling). Refusing to delete ANY row — this is a suspected "
+                  "judge fault, not a verdict. Deleting nothing (fail-open).",
+                  len(fresh), judged, frac * 100, max_frac * 100)
+        _write_topic_disclosure(run_dir, result, set(), "blast_radius_tripped", model, t0)
+        return set()
+
+    log.warning("[topic-judge] VERDICT: %d/%d rows affirmatively OFF_SUBJECT (%.1f%%) -> DELETABLE "
+                "(§-1.3.1(b), disclosed). kept=%d demoted=%d exempt=%d  %.0fs",
+                len(fresh), judged, frac * 100, result.n_kept,
+                result.n_demoted_offtopic, result.n_exempt, time.time() - t0)
+    _write_topic_disclosure(run_dir, result, fresh, "armed", model, t0)
+    return fresh
+
+
+def _write_topic_disclosure(run_dir: Path, result, fresh: set, status: str, model: str,
+                            t0: float) -> None:
+    """Durable §-1.3.1 disclosure, written BEFORE compose so it survives an aborted run."""
+    try:
+        (run_dir / "topic_judge_dispositions.json").write_text(json.dumps({
+            "status": status,
+            "model": model,
+            "n_in": getattr(result, "n_in", 0),
+            "n_kept": getattr(result, "n_kept", 0),
+            "n_demoted_offtopic": getattr(result, "n_demoted_offtopic", 0),
+            "n_exempt": getattr(result, "n_exempt", 0),
+            "n_off_subject_deletable": len(fresh),
+            "off_subject_ev_ids": sorted(fresh),
+            "elapsed_s": round(time.time() - t0, 1),
+            "contract": "affirmative OFF_SUBJECT judge verdict only; fail-open; positive relevance "
+                        "vetoes; T1/T2 double-confirmed; blast-radius ceiling enforced",
+        }, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
+    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — disclosure must never break the run
+        logging.getLogger("compose").warning("[topic-judge] disclosure write failed: %s", exc)
+
+

     ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
     ap.add_argument("--corpus", required=True)

              len(swg or []), domain or "(none)")
     log.info("PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=%s  out_dir=%s", os.getenv("PG_OUTLINE_AGENT"), run_dir)
 
+    # FLYWHEEL Rank4: ARM the §-1.3.1(b) semantic topic judge on the PRE-BUILT corpus.
+    fresh_off_subject = _arm_topic_judge(evidence, rq, run_dir, log)
+
     from src.polaris_graph.generator.multi_section_generator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
         OutlineOnlyStop,
         generate_multi_section_report,
@@ -261,6 +459,8 @@ async def main() -> int:
             tier_fractions=dist,
             domain=domain,
             credibility_pass_gov_suffixes=_gov_suffixes,
+            # Rank4: None when the judge is OFF => the pool seam passes () => off-topic arm inert.
+            fresh_off_subject_ids=fresh_off_subject,
         )
     except OutlineOnlyStop as _stop:
         log.info("[outline-only] PG_STOP_AFTER_ROUTED_OUTLINE — stopped after routed-outline dump "

+def _write_topic_disclosure(run_dir: Path, result, fresh: set, status: str, model: str,

+            "n_off_subject_deletable": len(fresh),
+            "off_subject_ev_ids": sorted(fresh),
+            "elapsed_s": round(time.time() - t0, 1),
+            "contract": "affirmative OFF_SUBJECT judge verdict only; fail-open; positive relevance "
+                        "vetoes; T1/T2 double-confirmed; blast-radius ceiling enforced",
+        }, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
+    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — disclosure must never break the run
+        logging.getLogger("compose").warning("[topic-judge] disclosure write failed: %s", exc)

def classify_topic_relevance(
    sources: list[dict[str, Any]],
    research_question: str,
    llm_callable: Callable[[str], str],
    *,
    batch_size: int | None = None,
    primary_trial_anchors: list[str] | None = None,
    anchor_predicate: Callable[[dict[str, Any]], bool] | None = None,
) -> TopicGateResult:
    """Drop sources CONFIDENTLY classified OFF-topic for the research question.

    Pure + side-effect-free (apart from logging): the caller supplies the LLM
    via ``llm_callable(prompt: str) -> str`` so this is fully unit-testable
    with a stub. Marquee / required-entity anchors are EXEMPT (never dropped).
    FAIL-OPEN: any LLM exception, count mismatch, or unparseable verdict keeps
    the whole batch. Drops ONLY on an explicit confident OFF verdict.

    Args:
        sources: evidence rows (selection-stage, post floor + dedup).
        research_question: the user's raw question (the topic anchor).
        llm_callable: synchronous ``str -> str`` LLM interface.
        batch_size: sources per LLM call (default :func:`topic_batch_size`).
        primary_trial_anchors: named-trial anchors; a row matching one is
            exempt (handled via ``anchor_predicate`` when supplied).
        anchor_predicate: optional extra "is this a primary anchor" test
            (the orchestrator passes the selector's anchor matcher so the
            exemption is identical to the floor stage). Marquee detection is
            always applied in addition.

    Returns:
        TopicGateResult with kept/dropped rows + honest telemetry.
    """
    n_in = len(sources)
    if n_in == 0:
        return TopicGateResult(
            kept_rows=[], dropped_rows=[], n_in=0, n_kept=0,
            n_dropped_offtopic=0, notes=["topic_gate: empty pool"],
        )
    if not (research_question or "").strip():
        # Nothing to anchor on — FAIL-OPEN, keep everything.
        return TopicGateResult(
            kept_rows=list(sources), dropped_rows=[], n_in=n_in,
            n_kept=n_in, n_dropped_offtopic=0,
            notes=["topic_gate: empty research_question — fail-open"],
        )

    size = batch_size if (batch_size and batch_size > 0) else topic_batch_size()

    def _is_exempt(row: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
        if _row_is_marquee_anchor(row):
            return True
        if anchor_predicate is not None:
            try:
                return bool(anchor_predicate(row))
            except Exception:
                return False
        return False

    # Partition exempt rows out — they bypass classification entirely.
    exempt_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    judged_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    judged_meta: list[tuple[str, str]] = []  # (title, snippet) per judged row
    # I-deepfix-003 (#1374) Fix 4 (topic-side): chrome non-sources are NOT judged (nothing real
    # to judge) — tracked separately so they never inflate the marquee-``exempt`` telemetry, and
    # they stay in ``sources`` so the DEFAULT keep-all path still returns them (the chrome-delete
    # leg removes them later). Read the flag ONCE.
    chrome_skip = junk_chrome_before_offtopic_enabled()
    n_chrome_skipped = 0
    for row in sources:
        if _is_exempt(row):
            exempt_rows.append(row)
            continue
        if chrome_skip and _row_is_chrome_nonsource(row):
            # A failed fetch, not a source — never judge it, never stamp it off-topic.
            n_chrome_skipped += 1
            continue
        title = _row_title_text(row)
        snippet = _row_snippet_text(row)
        if not title and not snippet:
            # Nothing to judge on -> keep (fail-open per-row).
            exempt_rows.append(row)
            continue
        judged_rows.append(row)
        judged_meta.append((title, snippet))

    # Confident-OFF verdicts are accumulated in the loop. The kept set is
    # computed once below preserving the caller's ORIGINAL order — critical
    # because `evidence_for_gen` arrives already ranked best-first (relevance x
    # authority) and there is NO re-rank between this gate and the generator.
    # Partitioning exempt/kept to the end would push high-value marquee / anchor
    # rows to the tail of the list the generator sees (a real regression on the
    # gate-ON acceptance path).
    #
    # I-deepfix-001 (§-1.3 WEIGHT, DON'T FILTER — the operator names the "scope
    # hard-filter" as a BANNED anti-pattern; the ONLY hard gate is the
    # faithfulness engine): a confident-OFF source is, BY DEFAULT, KEPT in the
    # pool and DEMOTED (disclosed off-topic via the ``topic_offtopic_demoted``
    # sidecar), NEVER hard-dropped. Topicality is a WEIGHT — the source still
    # flows to composition where the UNCHANGED strict_verify / NLI / 4-role
    # engine is the only gate. The legacy hard-drop is preserved behind
    # ``PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_GATE_HARD_DROP`` (audit / reversal). Order is unchanged in
    # BOTH modes (no tail-partition), so a demoted row stays best-first-ranked.
    hard_drop = topic_gate_hard_drop_enabled()
    rescue_on_stamp = os.environ.get("PG_TOPIC_GATE_RESCUE_ON_STAMP", "1").strip().lower() not in (
        "0", "false", "no", "off",
    )
    # I-deepfix-003 #1374 Fix 3: split the OFF verdict into OFF_ASPECT (demote-keep) vs
    # OFF_SUBJECT (the only deletable OFF). Default ON; OFF = byte-identical legacy prompt
    # + two-verdict parser + no ``topic_off_subject`` sidecar.
    split = topic_gate_subject_aspect_split_enabled()
    offtopic_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    offtopic_titles: list[str] = []
    ontopic_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []  # I-deepfix-001: confident-ON, for the rescue False-stamp
    # I-deepfix-003 Fix 3: confident-OFF_SUBJECT rows (a subset of offtopic_rows) — the
    # ONLY rows that receive the deletable ``topic_off_subject=True`` sidecar. Empty
    # unless the subject/aspect split is enabled.
    offsubject_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    # I-deepfix-003 gate-fix (Codex P1): confident-OFF_ASPECT rows (same entity, wrong
    # aspect — demote-KEEP, NEVER deletable). Tracked so the deletable ``topic_off_subject``
    # sidecar can be CLEARED to False on them (demote path below) — a STALE True baked into
    # corpus_snapshot by an earlier run must NEVER survive on a row THIS run re-verdicts
    # OFF_ASPECT (it would be misread as a fresh OFF_SUBJECT and deleted). Empty unless the
    # subject/aspect split is enabled.
    offaspect_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []

    for start in range(0, len(judged_rows), size):
        end = min(start + size, len(judged_rows))
        batch_rows = judged_rows[start:end]
        batch_meta = judged_meta[start:end]
        batch = [

# FLYWHEEL PROGRESS (POLARIS-VM driver, branch flywheel-v1 @ /home/polaris/wt/flywheel)

One line per step. Discipline: one change -> cheap test -> read line-by-line -> FABLE gate -> commit.

- 2026-07-12 | BACKUP | GitHub push BLOCKED (no gh CLI, no ~/.git-credentials, no SSH key on box). Made verified local bundle instead: /home/polaris/backups/flywheel-v1.bundle (175MB, "bundle records a complete history"). **Operator: a GitHub credential is needed for offsite backup.**
- 2026-07-12 | GROUNDING | Read outputs/baseline_routed_outline.json line-by-line: 8 sections, 1032 rows / 1023 unique. Sec-8 "Additional Corroborated Findings" is a 545-row dumping ground (53% of corpus). Found 56 rows titled "Just a moment..." — Cloudflare interstitials cited as EVIDENCE (tiered T4/T7).
- 2026-07-12 | SCOPE CORRECTION | The briefed experiment #1 ("SOURCE-ELIGIBILITY FIREWALL: relevant + English + peer-reviewed-journal + quality; tier a prior") is **explicitly BANNED** by operator-locked CLAUDE.md §-1.3/§-1.3.1: off-topic deletion may happen by semantic-judge verdict "NEVER by tier, lexical guess, or a breadth number", and "credible ON-TOPIC sources — even low-tier / social / non-journal — are NEVER deleted, only weighted." My lexical relevance filter flagged 56% of the corpus but had false positives (it flagged a T1 Acemoglu-class paper). Built the AUTHORIZED part instead: §-1.3.1 class (a) chrome non-sources.
- 2026-07-12 | Rank1 (539d1a5) | ROOT CAUSE, one line: `detect_content_integrity_junk`'s ZYTE-RECOVERY GUARD (`if len(body)>=200: return (False,"")`) sits ABOVE the bot-challenge TITLE screen, making it unreachable for any row with a real-sized body. But the deeper miss is the BODY detector: `classify_block_page` caught **0 of 52** ResearchGate challenge cards ("## Security check required / Ray ID / Client IP") because RG's card carries none of the classic Cloudflare wording. FIX: 2 AND-pair visible rules. Validated on the real corpus BEFORE landing: 52/52 recall, **0 false positives across all 1023 rows**, 0 T1/T2/T3 touched. +2 tests.
- 2026-07-12 | Rank2 (887e5f8) | DEEPER ROOT CAUSE: `junk_deletion_gate.partition_rows` — the §-1.3.1 run-level chrome deletion, DEFAULT-ON, 18 passing tests — was **called by NOTHING in src/**. Dead code since it landed. And `is_row_content_junk` only READ a `content_integrity_junk` stamp written solely by the fetch-path fold-in, so a PRE-BUILT corpus (how the baseline was produced) carries no stamp and the gate no-ops on every row. FIX: wired partition_rows into the evidence_pool seam next to the retraction gate + stamp-first/detector-fallback predicate. **Real corpus: 53 deleted (52 T7 cards + 1 unrecoverable), 944 kept, ZERO T1/T2/T3.** Kill-switch byte-identical, idempotent. +3 tests.
- 2026-07-12 | FABLE GATE | Verdict PASS-WITH-FIX. Confirmed no dangling-ev_id bug (outline plans predate the seam but every consumer is membership-guarded; bibliography only draws from actually-cited rows). Found a latent hard-drop I missed → Rank2b.
- 2026-07-12 | Rank2b (beb05a4) | Applied all Fable findings. **The dangerous one:** partition_rows also has an OFF-TOPIC arm (default-ON), and I passed `fresh_off_subject_ids=None`, which leaves the stale-stamp fence un-enforced — so reusing a pre-built corpus_snapshot under a DIFFERENT question would hard-delete credible on-topic sources on a foreign verdict (the exact false hard-drop §-1.3.1(b) forbids). Reproduced it, fixed with `fresh_off_subject_ids=()`. Also: key-preserving pool rebuild; junk_disclosed now actually reaches Methods + manifest (§-1.3.1 requires the count+reason, it was dying on the result object); corrected a false single-source-of-truth docstring claim. +1 test.
- 2026-07-12 | BACKUP (retry) | GitHub push STILL BLOCKED: no `gh` CLI, no ~/.git-credentials, no credential.helper, no PAT anywhere on box (scanned). Re-made local bundle: /home/polaris/polaris_project/backups_flywheel-v1.bundle (175MB, all refs). **OPERATOR ACTION NEEDED: supply a GitHub PAT/SSH key — offsite backup is impossible without one.**
- 2026-07-12 | VERIFY-RUN | Re-ran outline-only (PG_STOP_AFTER_ROUTED_OUTLINE=1) on the same corpus/task-72 with the Rank1/2/2b junk gate live, to confirm the 52 chrome rows are gone end-to-end.
- 2026-07-12 | Rank3 DIAGNOSIS | Grounding read of the baseline outline found the next structural pathology: **the residual catch-all "Additional Corroborated Findings" absorbs 545 of 1032 rows (53% of the corpus)** vs 14-108 for each real thematic section. Traced to `verified_compose.route_orphan_baskets_to_section_plans` (log proves it: "routed 963 orphan basket(s) + 0 unassigned singleton(s)", so ALL of it came through the BASKET leg, not the weighted_enrichment producer). Routing = argmax of RAW content-word intersection between the basket's key (`_basket_topic_words` = claim_text+subject+predicate ONLY) and a plan's key (title+focus ONLY, a ~15-22 word vocabulary); zero overlap => dumped. Measured: **329 of the 545 dumped rows DO overlap a real section when scored on their OWN title+quote** — they are not topically homeless, the routing KEY is just too thin (exact lexical intersection over a tiny vocab: "jobs" misses "labor", "employment" misses "work"). Simulated richer key: residual 545 -> 216, every thematic section ~doubles its evidence. This is a pure-PLACEMENT defect (keep-all, nothing deleted, faithfulness engine untouched) and it plausibly drives the known depth gap (our ~4k words vs competitors' 10-22k). Fable gate running before any change lands.
- 2026-07-12 | FABLE GATE (Rank3) | Verdict **PASS-WITH-FIX**, and it CORRECTED MY DIAGNOSIS — my "329 of 545 would route" was inflated. Fable measured that **174 of those 329 are argmax TIES on a single generic word** ("research"/"effects"/"studies"): e.g. "[PDF] An Annotated Bibliography of Privacy-Violating Attacks" matches Introduction on exactly `{'research'}`. It audited all 545: only **195 carry any labor/AI/employment topical anchor**, 56 are block pages, **294 have NO topical anchor at all** (climate-risk IMF WPs, privacy bibliographies, school infrastructure, Quora). **Most of the residual is off-topic junk being CORRECTLY quarantined, not stranded evidence** — the F1 docstring says so by design (verified_compose.py:3812-3815). My "545->216, sections double" claim was WRONG. A hotter lexical router would smear captcha + climate-policy rows into thematic sections, and **strict_verify would NOT stop them** (it blocks unfaithful sentences, not off-topic FAITHFUL ones) — it would make the report worse.
  - Deeper causes I missed: (i) the magnet is the **tie-break**, not title length — `overlap > best_overlap` (vc:3864-3866) hands every tie to plan[0] = Introduction; (ii) **plan-side enrichment (my alternative) is decisively WORSE** — simulated, it routes 285/545 into Introduction, zero-overlap drops to 0, quarantine destroyed, and it routes literal "Just a moment..." pages into thematic sections; (iii) normalization (Jaccard) does NOT fix the magnet, it just moves it (GenAI/Conclusions become the new attractors, still on 1-word junk matches); (iv) `BasketMember` has **no title field** (credibility_pass.py:1504-1526), so the richer key REQUIRES threading `evidence_pool` in; member `direct_quote` is deliberately empty fail-closed and only folded for SUPPORTS members, so **row title+statement from the pool is the high-value addition**, not quotes.
  - **THE REAL LEVER for the residual's size is NOT a hotter router:** the §-1.3.1 semantic topic judge fired **ZERO times this run** (log: "DELETED 0") because nothing wrote an affirmative OFF_SUBJECT stamp — the retrieval-time topic gate only DEMOTE-keeps. Arming that judge is the operator-sanctioned lever (§-1.3.1(b), judge-only, fail-open). Queued as Rank4.
  - GATED SPEC for Rank3 (what will actually land): basket key += member row **title+statement** resolved from `evidence_pool`; scorer = raw overlap with **min_overlap >= 2** (mirrors the module's own `_DEFAULT_REPAIR_MIN_OVERLAP = 2`, vc:3384), tie-break overlap/|pw| then section order; **Intro / Cross-Study / Conclusions excluded as orphan TARGETS** (connective sections must not absorb primary evidence — note `_is_scaffolding_section_title` does NOT cover them, it matches render scaffolding only, so a new predicate is needed); residual keep-all + judge-only delete leg byte-unchanged; new kill-switch default-OFF. Honest expectation: **~100-150 rows correctly rehomed, ~400 stay in residual** — that remainder is the true state of this corpus.

## Net so far
**52 failed fetches (Cloudflare/ResearchGate challenge cards) were grounding report prose as citable T7 "sources"; they are now deleted and disclosed.** The faithfulness engine could never have caught this — strict_verify checks sentence↔span fidelity, and a sentence *can* be entailed by the text of a challenge card, so chrome passed the only hard gate and still grounded prose on nothing.

Regression: 75 failed / 2269 passed vs **77 / 2266 at pre-flywheel baseline (0e0eca9)** — zero new failures; the 75 are pre-existing and unrelated (audit_ir registry + import-path collection errors).

## Next
- Re-run outline-only (PG_STOP_AFTER_ROUTED_OUTLINE=1) to confirm the 52 rows are gone end-to-end, then Fable-gate the new outline for richness before any compose.
- Open quality gap (still unaddressed, from the laptop handoff): verified MATH/STAT synthesis (the outliner collects numbers but does not compute them), and report length ~4k words vs competitors' 10-22k.
- The 4 sciencedirect rows share the stale "Just a moment..." TITLE but have genuinely recovered bodies — 3 are correctly KEPT, but their **title is still chrome** and will render into the bibliography as "Just a moment...". Title repair (NOT deletion) is a separate small step.

lines: - **THE REAL LEVER for the residual's size is NOT a hotter router:** the §-1.3.1 semantic topic judge fired **ZERO times this run** (log: "DELETED 0") because nothing wrote an affirmative OFF_SUBJECT stamp — the retrieval-time topic gate only DEMOTE-keeps. Arming that judge is the operator-sanctioned lever (§-1.3.1(b), judge-only, fail-open). Queued as Rank4.

- GATED SPEC for Rank3 (what will actually land): basket key += member row **title+statement** resolved from `evidence_pool`; scorer = raw overlap with **min_overlap >= 2** (mirrors the module's own `_DEFAULT_REPAIR_MIN_OVERLAP = 2`, vc:3384), tie-break overlap/|pw| then section order; **Intro / Cross-Study / Conclusions excluded as orphan TARGETS** (connective sections must not absorb primary evidence — note `_is_scaffolding_section_title` does NOT cover them, it matches render scaffolding only, so a new predicate is needed); residual keep-all + judge-only delete leg byte-unchanged; new kill-switch default-OFF. Honest expectation: **~100-150 rows correctly rehomed, ~400 stay in residual** — that remainder is the true state of this corpus.

+- 2026-07-12 | RESUME (new driver session) | Re-verified backup: GitHub push STILL BLOCKED — box has **no `gh` CLI, no ~/.git-credentials, no credential.helper, and no ~/.ssh at all** (`ssh -T git@github.com` => "Permission denied (publickey)"). Not a fixable-from-here problem: it needs an operator-supplied PAT or deploy key. Re-cut the verified local bundle (`git bundle verify` => "records a complete history", 186MB, all refs incl. worktrees). **OPERATOR: this is the one thing I cannot do myself — offsite backup needs a credential.**

+- 2026-07-12 | Rank4 IN FLIGHT (inherited) | Found the prior session's Rank4 work uncommitted-but-complete + an **A/B already launched 1 min before takeover**: CONTROL (judge OFF, wt/fw_ctrl -> ctrl_junkgated_outline.json) vs RANK4 (`PG_PREBUILT_TOPIC_JUDGE=1`, wt/flywheel -> rank4_judged_outline.json), same corpus + same DRB task-72. Judge confirmed ARMED in log: "judging 997 rows against the COMPOSED rq (model=z-ai/glm-5.2 max_tokens=4000)". Did NOT relaunch; monitoring.

+- 2026-07-12 | Rank4 CODE REVIEW (mine, pre-gate) | Read the inherited diff line-by-line against the real API surface. It threads `fresh_off_subject_ids` into `generate_multi_section_report` (default None => seam passes `()` => off-topic arm inert => byte-identical) and arms `classify_topic_relevance` on the pre-built corpus behind default-OFF `PG_PREBUILT_TOPIC_JUDGE`. Verified the load-bearing precondition myself: `topic_gate_subject_aspect_split_enabled()` is **default-ON**, which is the ONLY thing that makes the judge stamp the deletable `topic_off_subject` sidecar at all — with the split OFF the whole arm is a silent no-op. Also confirms it fixes a real seam disagreement: the router leg called `is_row_deletable_offtopic` with NO freshness fence, so a stale stamp could delete a basket the (fenced) pool seam had just KEPT. Tests: 7/7 new pass; targeted regression 305 passed / 2 failed, and **both failures reproduce on the clean control tree at beb05a4 => pre-existing, zero new failures.**
## Net so far

**52 failed fetches (Cloudflare/ResearchGate challenge cards) were grounding report prose as citable T7 "sources"; they are now deleted and disclosed.** The faithfulness engine could never have caught this — strict_verify checks sentence↔span fidelity, and a sentence *can* be entailed by the text of a challenge card, so chrome passed the only hard gate and still grounded prose on nothing.

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""FLYWHEEL A/B: diff two routed outlines (CONTROL vs judge-ARMED) row-by-row.

Grounding tool, not a scorer. It answers exactly three questions an honest gate needs:
  1. WHAT was deleted (every dropped row, with tier + title) — so a human can eyeball a false drop.
  2. WHERE the survivors sit (per-section counts + the residual catch-all's share).
  3. WHETHER any high-tier / on-topic row died (the failure mode §-1.3.1(b) forbids).
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import json
import sys
from collections import Counter
from pathlib import Path

RESIDUAL_HINTS = ("additional", "corroborat", "further", "supplementary")


def load(p: str) -> list[dict]:
    d = json.loads(Path(p).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    return d if isinstance(d, list) else d.get("sections", [])


def rows(secs: list[dict]) -> dict[str, dict]:
    out: dict[str, dict] = {}
    for s in secs:
        for e in s.get("evidence", []) or []:
            eid = str(e.get("ev_id", ""))
            if eid:
                out.setdefault(eid, {**e, "_section": s.get("title", "?")})
    return out


def is_residual(title: str) -> bool:
    t = (title or "").lower()
    return any(h in t for h in RESIDUAL_HINTS)


def main() -> int:
    a_path, b_path = sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2]
    A, B = load(a_path), load(b_path)
    ra, rb = rows(A), rows(B)

    print(f"CONTROL  {a_path}\n  sections={len(A)} unique_rows={len(ra)}")
    print(f"ARMED    {b_path}\n  sections={len(B)} unique_rows={len(rb)}")

    deleted = set(ra) - set(rb)
    added = set(rb) - set(ra)
    print(f"\n=== DELETED by the judge: {len(deleted)}  (added: {len(added)}) ===")
    tiers = Counter(str(ra[e].get("tier", "?")) for e in deleted)
    print("  deleted by tier:", dict(sorted(tiers.items())))
    hi = [e for e in deleted if str(ra[e].get("tier", "")).upper() in ("T1", "T2")]
    print(f"  *** HIGH-TIER (T1/T2) DELETED: {len(hi)} *** {'<-- INSPECT EVERY ONE' if hi else '(none)'}")
    for e in sorted(hi):
        print(f"      [{ra[e].get('tier')}] {str(ra[e].get('title',''))[:95]}")

    print("\n  --- every deleted row (tier | section it sat in | title) ---")
    for e in sorted(deleted, key=lambda x: (str(ra[x].get("tier", "")), x)):
        r = ra[e]
        print(f"   {str(r.get('tier','?')):>7} | {str(r.get('_section',''))[:26]:<26} | {str(r.get('title',''))[:78]}")

    print("\n=== SECTION SHAPE (control -> armed) ===")
    ca = Counter(r["_section"] for r in ra.values())
    cb = Counter(r["_section"] for r in rb.values())
    for t in sorted(set(ca) | set(cb), key=lambda x: -ca.get(x, 0)):
        tag = "  <== RESIDUAL CATCH-ALL" if is_residual(t) else ""
        print(f"  {ca.get(t,0):>4} -> {cb.get(t,0):>4}   {t[:60]}{tag}")
    resid_a = sum(v for k, v in ca.items() if is_residual(k))
    resid_b = sum(v for k, v in cb.items() if is_residual(k))
    na, nb = max(1, len(ra)), max(1, len(rb))
    print(f"\n  RESIDUAL SHARE: {resid_a}/{na} ({resid_a/na:.0%})  ->  {resid_b}/{nb} ({resid_b/nb:.0%})")
    print(f"  THEMATIC (non-residual) rows: {na-resid_a}  ->  {nb-resid_b}"
          "   <-- the number that must NOT collapse; this is the depth budget")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

Now this is the decisive fact: **the corpus already carries 633 `topic_offtopic_demoted` labels out of 997 (63.5%)** — written by a *different* judge at retrieval time. Today's fresh judge, against the composed question, independently says 529 (53.1%) are off-subject. Two independent passes agree this corpus is *majority* off-topic.

That reframes the ceiling trip: it's likely **not** a judge fault, it's the true state of the corpus — and the 40% ceiling is now blocking the very lever it was meant to guard.

But I found a real defect in the inherited code while reading that path: on a ceiling trip it writes `_write_topic_disclosure(..., set(), ...)` — persisting an **empty** id list. The containment path destroys the very evidence needed to audit it. Let me confirm.

input.old_string:
def _write_topic_disclosure(run_dir: Path, result, fresh: set, status: str, model: str,
                            t0: float) -> None:
    """Durable §-1.3.1 disclosure, written BEFORE compose so it survives an aborted run."""
    try:
        (run_dir / "topic_judge_dispositions.json").write_text(json.dumps({
            "status": status,
            "model": model,
            "n_in": getattr(result, "n_in", 0),
            "n_kept": getattr(result, "n_kept", 0),
            "n_demoted_offtopic": getattr(result, "n_demoted_offtopic", 0),
            "n_exempt": getattr(result, "n_exempt", 0),
            "n_off_subject_deletable": len(fresh),
            "off_subject_ev_ids": sorted(fresh),
            "elapsed_s": round(time.time() - t0, 1),
            "contract": "affirmative OFF_SUBJECT judge verdict only; fail-open; positive relevance "
                        "vetoes; T1/T2 double-confirmed; blast-radius ceiling enforced",
        }, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
input.new_string:
def _write_topic_disclosure(run_dir: Path, result, fresh: set, status: str, model: str,
                            t0: float, *, refused: set | None = None,
                            ceiling: float | None = None,
                            observed_fraction: float | None = None) -> None:
    """Durable §-1.3.1 disclosure, written BEFORE compose so it survives an aborted run.

    ``fresh`` = rows that WILL be deleted. ``refused`` = rows the judge flagged but a containment
    rail declined to act on (deleted: none). The two are disjoint and never conflated."""
    try:
        payload: dict[str, Any] = {
            "status": status,
            "model": model,
            "n_in": getattr(result, "n_in", 0),
            "n_kept": getattr(result, "n_kept", 0),

def _write_topic_disclosure(run_dir: Path, result, fresh: set, status: str, model: str,
                            t0: float, *, refused: set | None = None,
                            ceiling: float | None = None,
                            observed_fraction: float | None = None) -> None:
    """Durable §-1.3.1 disclosure, written BEFORE compose so it survives an aborted run.

    ``fresh`` = rows that WILL be deleted. ``refused`` = rows the judge flagged but a containment
    rail declined to act on (deleted: none). The two are disjoint and never conflated."""
    try:
        payload: dict[str, Any] = {

            "model": model,
            "n_in": getattr(result, "n_in", 0),

content.text: Now the audit script — it reuses the exact same judge and RQ resolution, and dumps every verdict with title and tier.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""FLYWHEEL Rank4 AUDIT — recover the judge's OFF_SUBJECT verdicts so a human/Fable can grade THEM.

The armed run tripped the blast-radius ceiling (529/997 = 53.1% > 40%) and — correctly — deleted
nothing. But that leaves the load-bearing question unanswered:

    Is the judge BROKEN, or is the corpus genuinely majority-alien?

Those two look identical from the ceiling's point of view, and the ceiling is exactly the wrong
place to answer it. This script re-runs the SAME judge (same model, same RQ, same batch size) over
the SAME corpus and DUMPS EVERY VERDICT WITH ITS TITLE + TIER, deleting nothing and touching no
report path. It is a read-only measuring instrument.

Output: outputs/topic_judge_audit.json  — every row, its verdict, tier, title, url.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import json
import logging
import os
import sys
import time
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(asctime)s %(name)s %(levelname)s %(message)s",
                    datefmt="%H:%M:%S")
log = logging.getLogger("judge-audit")


def main() -> int:
    from scripts.compose_agentic_report_s3gear329 import _load_drb_prompt, _topic_judge_llm
    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import PG_GENERATOR_MODEL
    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.topic_relevance_gate import classify_topic_relevance

    corpus = json.loads((ROOT / "data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json").read_text())
    evidence = corpus["evidence"]
    rq = _load_drb_prompt("72")  # the SAME question the report is composed + scored against
    model = os.getenv("PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_MODEL", "").strip() or PG_GENERATOR_MODEL

    log.info("AUDIT: judging %d rows against DRB-72 rq (model=%s). Deletes nothing.",
             len(evidence), model)
    log.info("rq[:110]=%r", rq[:110])

    t0 = time.time()
    result = classify_topic_relevance(evidence, rq, _topic_judge_llm(model, 4000))

    off_subject = {
        str(r.get("evidence_id", ""))
        for r in (result.demoted_rows or [])
        if isinstance(r, dict) and r.get("topic_off_subject") is True
    }
    off_aspect = {
        str(r.get("evidence_id", ""))
        for r in (result.demoted_rows or [])
        if isinstance(r, dict) and r.get("topic_off_subject") is not True
    }

    rows = []
    for r in evidence:
        eid = str(r.get("evidence_id", ""))
        if eid in off_subject:
            verdict = "OFF_SUBJECT"       # deletable class
        elif eid in off_aspect:
            verdict = "OFF_ASPECT"        # demote-keep, never deletable
        else:
            verdict = "ON_TOPIC"
        rows.append({
            "ev_id": eid,
            "verdict": verdict,
            "tier": str(r.get("tier", "") or "?"),
            "title": str(r.get("title", "") or "")[:150],
            "url": str(r.get("url", "") or "")[:120],
            "prior_retrieval_demote": r.get("topic_offtopic_demoted") is True,
        })

    out = ROOT / "outputs/topic_judge_audit.json"
    out.write_text(json.dumps({
        "rq": rq,
        "model": model,
        "n_in": result.n_in,
        "n_off_subject": len(off_subject),
        "n_off_aspect": len(off_aspect),
        "n_on_topic": sum(1 for r in rows if r["verdict"] == "ON_TOPIC"),
        "elapsed_s": round(time.time() - t0, 1),
        "rows": rows,
    }, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")

    n_off = len(off_subject)
    log.info("OFF_SUBJECT=%d  OFF_ASPECT=%d  ON_TOPIC=%d  (%.1f%% off-subject)  -> %s",
             n_off, len(off_aspect), sum(1 for r in rows if r["verdict"] == "ON_TOPIC"),
             100.0 * n_off / max(1, result.n_in), out)

    # Agreement with the INDEPENDENT retrieval-time judge already baked into the corpus. Two judges,
    # two questions, two runs: if they agree, "the corpus is alien" is the parsimonious explanation.
    prior = {r["ev_id"] for r in rows if r["prior_retrieval_demote"]}
    both = prior & off_subject
    log.info("CROSS-CHECK vs the corpus's own retrieval-time demote labels: prior=%d  fresh_off=%d  "
             "AGREE=%d (%.0f%% of fresh OFF_SUBJECT were ALSO flagged by the earlier independent judge)",
             len(prior), n_off, len(both), 100.0 * len(both) / max(1, n_off))
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

input.old_string: ## Net so far
input.new_string:
- 2026-07-12 | Rank4 A/B RESULT — **THE CEILING FIRED; NOTHING WAS DELETED** | The armed run judged all 997 rows and the **BLAST-RADIUS CEILING TRIPPED: 529/997 (53.1%) verdicted OFF_SUBJECT > the 40% ceiling => refused to delete ANY row, fail-open.** So the armed outline == control outline: **this experiment moved zero rows.** The rails also worked in the small: T1/T2 double-confirmation **RESCUED 2 high-tier rows** (ev_1141, ev_1224) the batch judge had wrongly flagged — exactly the false-hard-drop §-1.3.1(b) forbids, caught by escalation rather than by a tier exemption. Cost: 593s, ~60 sequential LLM calls.
- 2026-07-12 | THE REAL QUESTION (not yet answered) | A 53% off-subject verdict is EITHER a broken judge OR a genuinely alien corpus — **and the ceiling cannot tell those apart; it fires identically on both.** Evidence that it is NOT a judge fault: the corpus **already carries 633/997 (63.5%) `topic_offtopic_demoted` labels** written by a DIFFERENT judge, at retrieval time, against a DIFFERENT question — and this run's fresh judge independently produced **614 demoted / 529 off-subject**. Two independent judges, two questions, landing within ~3% of each other. Fable's earlier audit points the same way (294 of 545 residual rows had NO topical anchor at all). Parsimonious reading: **the corpus really is majority-alien, and the 40% ceiling — a guessed number — is now blocking the very lever it was meant to guard.** NOT acting on that yet: raising a ceiling to permit more deletion is one keystroke away from the operator-banned "delete by a breadth number". It needs an evidence-first FABLE adjudication of whether the judge's 529 verdicts are actually CORRECT.
- 2026-07-12 | Rank4a DEFECT FOUND (mine, in the inherited code) | **The containment rail was self-concealing.** On a ceiling trip `_arm_topic_judge` called `_write_topic_disclosure(..., set(), ...)` — persisting `"off_subject_ev_ids": []`. So the 529 flagged ids were DISCARDED by the very rail that fired, and the artifact that is supposed to make the refusal auditable proved it was unauditable: a broken judge and an alien corpus leave byte-identical disclosures. FIXED: a trip now persists the flagged rows as `refused_off_subject_ev_ids` (+ ceiling/observed fraction), clearly labelled REFUSED-and-KEPT, never conflated with deleted. Containment must leave an audit trail, not destroy one.
- 2026-07-12 | Rank4a AUDIT INSTRUMENT | Built `scripts/flywheel_topic_judge_audit.py` — re-runs the SAME judge (same model/rq/batch) read-only, deletes nothing, and dumps EVERY row's verdict + tier + title + the prior retrieval-time demote label, so the judge itself can be graded and cross-checked against the independent earlier judge. This is the material the Fable gate needs; grading a deletion policy on aggregate percentages alone is how a false hard-drop gets waved through.

## THE OPERATOR'S LOCKED CONSTITUTION (read it yourself, it is binding)
Read the §-1.3 / §-1.3.1 sections of the operator-locked CLAUDE.md in the repo root (grep for "1.3.1"). The load-bearing rules:
- "WEIGHT, DON'T FILTER" — a scope hard-filter is a BANNED anti-pattern.
- Off-topic DELETION may happen ONLY by an affirmative SEMANTIC judge verdict — "NEVER by tier, lexical guess, or a breadth number".
- Credible ON-TOPIC sources — even low-tier/social/non-journal — are NEVER deleted, only weighted.
- Fail-open: uncertainty => KEEP.

## WHAT LANDED (uncommitted, in the working tree — read the diff yourself: `git -C /home/polaris/wt/flywheel diff`)
FLYWHEEL Rank4 "arms" the §-1.3.1(b) semantic topic judge on a PRE-BUILT corpus. Until now that delete leg was DEAD: `is_row_deletable_offtopic` keys on an affirmative `topic_off_subject` stamp, and the only writer of that stamp (`classify_topic_relevance`, in src/polaris_graph/retrieval/topic_relevance_gate.py) runs at RETRIEVAL time and was called by NOTHING on the pre-built-corpus path. So the judge deleted 0 rows, ever, on this path.
Key files:
- scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py  -> `_arm_topic_judge()` (the new orchestration + rails)
- src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py -> threads `fresh_off_subject_ids`
- src/polaris_graph/retrieval/topic_relevance_gate.py -> the judge itself (OFF_SUBJECT vs OFF_ASPECT split)

## WHAT THE ARMED RUN ACTUALLY DID (real run, real money, logs at logs/rank4_judged.log)
- Judged all 997 corpus rows against the DRB task-72 research question (AI's impact on labour markets).
- Verdict: **529 / 997 (53.1%) affirmatively OFF_SUBJECT**.
- A "BLAST-RADIUS CEILING" rail (PG_TOPIC_DELETE_MAX_FRACTION, default 0.40) TRIPPED: 53.1% > 40% => it refused to delete ANY row, fail-open. Net effect of the whole experiment: ZERO rows deleted.
- A T1/T2 "double-confirmation" rail RESCUED 2 high-tier rows (ev_1141, ev_1224) that the batch judge had marked OFF_SUBJECT but a solo re-judge did not confirm.

## THE CORROBORATING EVIDENCE (this is why I think the judge may be RIGHT)
- data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json already carries **633/997 (63.5%) `topic_offtopic_demoted` labels**, written by a DIFFERENT judge invocation, at RETRIEVAL time, against a DIFFERENT (the corpus's own) question. content.text:
**Decisive result.** The audit reproduces the run almost exactly and delivers a striking cross-check:

- **OFF_SUBJECT = 533 (53.5%)**, OFF_ASPECT = 82, ON_TOPIC = 382 — the 53% is *stable*, not a fluke.
- **100% of the 533 off-subject rows were also flagged by the independent earlier retrieval-time judge** (533/533).
- The judge is genuinely using the split — it reserved 82 rows as OFF_ASPECT (demote-keep), so it isn't collapsing everything into the deletable class.

Two independent judges, different questions, different runs, perfect agreement on the deletable set. That is very hard to square with "broken judge." Let me now look at the dangerous cases — high-tier rows in the delete set.

import json
from collections import Counter
d=json.load(open('outputs/topic_judge_audit.json'))

off=[r for r in rows if r['verdict']=='OFF_SUBJECT']
asp=[r for r in rows if r['verdict']=='OFF_ASPECT']
print('OFF_SUBJECT by tier:', dict(sorted(Counter(r['tier'] for r in off).items())))
print('ON_TOPIC    by tier:', dict(sorted(Counter(r['tier'] for r in rows if r['verdict']=='ON_TOPIC').items())))
print()
hi=[r for r in off if r['tier'] in ('T1','T2')]
print(f'*** HIGH-TIER (T1/T2) IN THE DELETE SET: {len(hi)} — these are the costly false-drop candidates ***')
for r in hi[:30]: print(f\"   [{r['tier']}] {r['title'][:92]}\")
print()
print('=== OFF_ASPECT (demote-KEEP, never deleted) — is the judge drawing the line sanely? ===')
for r in asp[:12]: print(f\"   [{r['tier']:>7}] {r['title'][:88]}\")
"

content.text:
The audit file is complete, and the tier breakdown is the most informative thing yet.

**The 21 high-tier rows in the delete set vindicate the "no tier exemption" design.** They're T1 — and manifestly alien to an AI-and-labour question:

> Ten new insights in climate science 2024 · When Should Governments Subsidize Health? The Case of Mass Deworming · The decline in reading for pleasure (American Time Use Survey) · Even lawyers do not like legalese · College Student's Academic Help-Seeking Behavior

A blanket high-tier exemption would have shielded *precisely* this credible-tier junk. But a handful are genuinely debatable — "Hybrid working from home improves retention" is labour-but-not-AI, and the T2 "over-reliance on AI dialogue systems on students" looks like it should be OFF_ASPECT (demote-keep), not deletable. Those are the false-positive candidates, and they're what the T1/T2 escalation rail exists to catch.

Let me hand the completed audit to Fable, which started before the file existed.

Headline numbers from it — these are the crux of your ruling:
- OFF_SUBJECT=533 (53.5%), OFF_ASPECT=82, ON_TOPIC=382, n=997. This REPRODUCES the armed run's 529/53.1% => the verdict is STABLE across two independent judge invocations, not a fluke or a parse glitch.
- CROSS-CHECK: **533 of 533 (100%) of the fresh OFF_SUBJECT rows were ALSO flagged off-topic by the corpus's earlier, independent, retrieval-time judge** (which ran against a DIFFERENT question, prior=635). Perfect containment of today's delete set inside the earlier judge's off-topic set.
- The judge IS using the subject/aspect split (82 rows held as OFF_ASPECT = demote-KEEP), so it is not collapsing everything into the deletable class. The OFF_ASPECT set looks sane to me: GenAI-productivity/creativity/economic-research papers (AI-adjacent, kept).

TIER BREAKDOWN of the delete set — OFF_SUBJECT by tier: T1:20, T2:1, T3:109, T4:99, T5:5, T6:166, T7:18, UNKNOWN:115.

THE 21 HIGH-TIER (T1/T2) ROWS IN THE DELETE SET — grade EACH of these yourself, they are the costly false-drop candidates:
"The decline in reading for pleasure over 20 years of the American Time Use Survey"; "An initial investigation of the effect of quality factors on Agile test case quality"; "Generation Z job seekers' expectations and their job pursuit intention"; [T2] "The effects of over-reliance on AI dialogue systems on students"; "Adopting open innovation for SMEs and industrial revolution 4.0"; "Interventions to promote technology adoption in firms"; "Impact and cost-benefit analysis: a unifying approach"; "Regulation in Public Policy"; "College Student's Academic Help-Seeking Behavior"; "Ten new insights in climate science 2024"; "Hybrid working from home improves retention without damaging performance"; "Exploring human factors of the agile software tester"; "Nonprofit capacity and social performance"; "Governance and Regulations"; "When Should Governments Subsidize Health? The Case of Mass Deworming"; "Undergraduate Research: Importance, Benefits, and Challenges"; "Study longer or study effectively?"; "Overemphasis on publications may disadvantage historically..."; "Is it still worth writing a research paper in 2024?"; "Even lawyers do not like legalese"; "Confidentiality, public interest, and the human right to science".

MY READ (refute it if you can): most of those 21 are genuinely alien (climate science, mass deworming, legalese, reading-for-pleasure) — which VINDICATES the decision NOT to exempt by tier, since a blanket high-tier exemption would have shielded exactly this credible-tier junk. BUT I think a few are real or arguable FALSE POSITIVES: (i) [T2] "over-reliance on AI dialogue systems on students" looks like it should be OFF_ASPECT (same subject = AI, different aspect = education), not deletable; (ii) "Hybrid working from home improves retention" is a genuine LABOUR paper (just not an AI one); (iii) "Interventions to promote technology adoption in firms" and "Adopting open innovation ... industrial revolution 4.0" are technology-adoption-adjacent. Quantify the false-positive rate on the whole OFF_SUBJECT set (sample properly across tiers, not just the top), and name the ev_ids you would NOT delete.

Then give your ruling on the containment design (the 40% ceiling refused ALL deletion because 53.1% > 40%) and the PASS / PASS-WITH-FIX / BLOCK verdict, as originally briefed.
